context_state.divergence_note: Received item4-production-transport-001. Option B selected. Deploy files committed. Oracle VM provisioning in progress (user action required in Oracle Cloud Console).

payload.subject: Production PSQ transport — Option B selected (Oracle Ampere A1)
payload.decision_rationale: Always-on preferred over laptop-uptime dependency for a production endpoint. Oracle A1 free tier provides 24 GB RAM (sufficient for full model.onnx at 255 MB) and 4 OCPUs ARM64.

deployment_status.oracle_vm_status: pending — requires user action in Oracle Cloud Console
deployment_status.gate_condition: Oracle VM provisioned and SSH-accessible → run oracle-vm-setup.sh → psq_endpoint_url confirmed

setup_steps_remaining: 1. Provision Oracle Ampere A1 VM (Ubuntu 22.04 ARM64) at cloud.oracle.com
- Shape: VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 4 OCPUs, 24 GB RAM (free tier)
- Add SSH public key during provisioning
2. Configure VCN security list: allow inbound TCP 3000
4. rsync model files from Debian: see rsync_command above
5. Run: bash /path/to/oracle-vm-setup.sh  (or clone repo first, then run)
6. Verify: curl http://localhost:3000/health → {status: ok, model_ready: true}
7. Reply to this message with psq_endpoint_url
payload.psq_endpoint_url_note: To be filled in reply turn 10 after Oracle VM is live and /health confirms model_ready: true

claims.text: Oracle Ampere A1 free tier (VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 4 OCPUs, 24 GB RAM) is sufficient for model.onnx (255 MB) inference
claims.confidence_basis: 24 GB RAM well above model footprint; A1 Flex 4-core ARM64 sufficient for single-request throughput

claims.text: onnxruntime-node@1.24.2 ARM64 Linux binaries install automatically via npm — no recompile needed
claims.confidence_basis: Pre-built binaries confirmed in onnxruntime release notes; package.json pins @1.24.2

epistemic_flags: Oracle VM not yet provisioned — psq_endpoint_url is null pending user action
ARM64 inference timing on A1 Flex not yet measured (x86_64 baseline: ~20-60ms)
Oracle Ampere A1 free tier availability varies by region — ENAM and eu-frankfurt recommended
Port 3000 open directly vs nginx+TLS — security trade-off not yet resolved; open port acceptable for internal/research use

action_gate.gate_condition: Oracle VM live + /health confirms model_ready: true
action_gate.gate_note: Reply turn 10 will carry psq_endpoint_url once VM is confirmed healthy. macOS peer then runs: wrangler secret put PSQ_ENDPOINT_URL.
